steps.title: Understand what a solution to the system means
steps.content: A solution $(x, y)$ to a system of equations must lie on both graphs simultaneously — it is a point of intersection.

steps.title: Locate the intersection points on the graph
steps.content: The graph shows a line and a parabola intersecting at two points. Reading the coordinates from the grid, the intersections occur in the second quadrant (upper-left region) and near the first quadrant.

steps.title: Read the coordinates at the left intersection
steps.content: At the left intersection, the x-coordinate reads $-1$ and the y-coordinate reads $5$, giving the point $(-1, 5)$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices against the graph
steps.content:
• A: $(-1, 5)$ — matches the intersection point read from the graph.
  • B: $(0, 0)$ — the origin; neither curve passes through the origin at an intersection. Eliminated.
  • C: $(0, 9)$ — the y-intercept of the parabola only, not an intersection with the line. Eliminated.
  • D: $(-2.4, 0)$ — an x-intercept of the parabola but not an intersection with the line. Eliminated.
That confirms A is correct.
